name: Ensure used KMSKeys are not exposed through publicly accessible VMs
description: Datastore encryption keys reachable through roles attached to publicly accessible virtual machines.
body:
### Overview

Cyscale fails this control when a KMS key used by a datastore can be reached through permissions attached to a VM that is publicly accessible on management ports. The check follows VM exposure, IAM role permissions, and KMS key relationships.

Fixing either the public VM exposure or the excessive key permission path will clear the issue.
remediationDescription:
## Remediation

Break the path from the public VM to the datastore encryption key.

1. Remove public SSH/RDP exposure from the VM security group, NSG, firewall, or equivalent rule.
2. Remove KMS decrypt/admin permissions from the VM role or service account if the VM does not need the key.
3. Scope key permissions to the datastore service principal or a dedicated workload identity.
4. If the VM must remain public, move it behind bastion or private access and use a separate least-privilege role.
5. Re-run sync and assessment.

Examples:

```bash
aws ec2 revoke-security-group-ingress --group-id <security-group-id> --protocol tcp --port 22 --cidr 0.0.0.0/0 --region {{asset.region}}
aws iam detach-role-policy --role-name <vm-role-name> --policy-arn <broad-kms-policy-arn>
```

Cyscale will pass when the exposed VM no longer has a permission path to the datastore KMS key, or when the VM is no longer publicly accessible.
